clinical_note: Penicillin allergy is part of the broader beta-lactam allergy review space.
Cross-reactivity is possible, especially with immediate severe reactions or similar side chains; requires pharmacist review.
Aspirin/salicylate hypersensitivity can cross-react with non-selective NSAIDs.
Treat as beta-lactam review, not automatic contraindication; CDC notes carbapenems can be safe in selected patients without recent IgE-mediated penicillin symptoms.
Do not auto-contraindicate: AAAAI states no clinically significant immune-mediated cross-reactivity between sulfonamide antibiotics and non-antibiotic sulfonamides.
Do not auto-contraindicate: loop diuretics such as furosemide are non-antibiotic sulfonamides and should not be blocked solely because of sulfonamide antibiotic allergy.
ACE-inhibitor angioedema should prompt same-class avoidance/review rather than switching blindly to another ACE inhibitor.
source: DailyMed/OpenFDA label evidence + NICE drug-allergy documentation

clinical_note: Suspected allergy to a non-selective NSAID should trigger avoidance/review of other non-selective NSAIDs.
